result.question: How much of my portfolio is in semiconductors?

data.text: Based on the portfolio snapshot you provided, none
of the listed holdings are pure‑play semiconductor stocks.
The only position that could contain semiconductor exposure
is the **Technology Select Sector SPDR® Fund (XLK)**,
which holds a broad basket of technology companies—including
some semiconductor firms—but the portfolio data does not
break out the sector composition or the weight

of semiconductors within that ETF.

Therefore:

| Holding

| Direct semiconductor exposure |
|---------|------------------------------|
| GOOGL

(Alphabet Inc.) | No |
| PPA (Invesco

Aerospace & Defense ETF) | No |
|
XLE (Energy Select Sector SPDR® Fund) | No

|
| VTV (Vanguard Value Index Fund ETF)

| No |
| XLK (Technology Select Sector
SPDR® Fund) | Indirect (contains semiconductors as part

of a broader tech basket) |
| XLV

(… ) | No |

**Result:** 0 %
of the portfolio is directly invested in semiconductor
companies; any semiconductor exposure is indirect and limited
to the XLK ETF, for which the exact
percentage cannot be determined from the current portfolio
data. [1]

I need to check your portfolio holdings first, then determine which are semiconductor companies.

Let me look up your portfolio and the semiconductor industry.
